title: The End of the Tunnel — Case File 3: The King Hotel Envelope
description: Case File 3 — Junior Detective: The third chapter follows the missing page to a sealed King Hotel envelope and a new access route.

answerKey.method: A cyanide-laced envelope seal handled during the private document reveal
answerKey.motive: To stop the envelope from proving Julian had been selling access through the King Hotel side
answerKey.keyEvidence: wax residue on Julian’s gloves, the room-service stamp, and the access-card duplicate
answerKey.explanation: Julian March is responsible. wax residue on Julian’s gloves, the room-service stamp, and the access-card duplicate proves the method and timeline. This chapter reveals: The King Hotel side was not a dead end; it was the modern entrance to the operation.

publicClues.title: Case File 3: The King Hotel Envelope
publicClues.text: Case File 3: The King Hotel Envelope begins inside The Tunnel. This is one chapter in a larger story, not a separate remake of the same mystery.

publicClues.title: The New Death
publicClues.text: The victim is Andre Lavigne. The old Peacock Alley legend still matters, but this chapter adds a deeper layer to the ongoing investigation.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 1
phone.text: Andre called Julian to ask why the King Hotel room number was still active.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 2
phone.text: Julian returned the call only after Andre said he had a duplicate access-card number.

messages.title: Messages Evidence 1
messages.text: Andre texted: “The envelope seal is newer than the paper.”

messages.title: Messages Evidence 2
messages.text: A reply from Julian said: “Open it in front of everyone. That proves chain of custody.”

maps.title: Maps Evidence 1
maps.text: Andre’s last location is the small table beside the King Hotel display case.

maps.title: Maps Evidence 2
maps.text: Julian’s route loops behind the display case during the guest toast.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 1
bank.text: A recurring payment from a private events account is labeled “historic corridor consulting.”

bank.title: Bank Evidence 2
bank.text: The payment amount matches access-card deposits tied to Julian’s office.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 1
photos.text: A photo shows Julian wearing thin gloves while moving the envelope.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 2
photos.text: Another image shows wax dust on the cuff of his jacket after Andre collapses.

social.title: Social Evidence 1
social.text: A post advertises an “exclusive King Hotel passage experience” before approval was granted.

social.title: Social Evidence 2
social.text: The post is removed after Andre mentions duplicate cards.

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 2
contacts.text: A note under the contact reads: “He says sealed, system says active.”

notes.title: Notes Evidence 1
notes.text: Detective note: the poison was not on the letter. It was on the seal Andre was told to break.

notes.title: Notes Evidence 2
notes.text: Detective note: the envelope matters because it proves the King Hotel side is operational.

files.title: Files Evidence 1
files.text: The access-card file shows two cards with the same credential name.

files.title: Files Evidence 2
files.text: The duplicate was printed from Julian’s administrator login.

browser.title: Browser Evidence 1
browser.text: A search asks whether cyanide residue can be hidden in colored wax.

browser.title: Browser Evidence 2
browser.text: Another search asks how to authenticate a 1920s hotel room-service stamp.

rounds.title: Final Accusation
rounds.objective: Close Case File 3: The King Hotel Envelope and reveal how it continues The End of the Tunnel arc.

openingNarration: Detectives, welcome to Case File 3: The King Hotel Envelope. Page 47 led investigators to a sealed envelope marked KING HOTEL — DO NOT DELIVER. Before document courier Andre Lavigne can open it safely, he dies during the reveal. The chapter is harder because the envelope is both evidence and weapon.
revealScript: Julian March killed Andre Lavigne because the King Hotel envelope exposed his duplicate access-card system. He used the old room-service legend as cover while selling modern access through the King side. The larger story now has a route, a code, and a gatekeeper.
